description:
Modify an existing building design into a highly transparent greenhouse, set the simulation date to the Winter Solstice, and generate a daily solar radiation heat map to evaluate winter sunlight penetration.

Expected Actions:
1. Open the existing building model located at `~/Documents/Energy3D/building-shape.ng3`.
2. Change the geographic location of the simulation to "Denver, CO" (a major US greenhouse hub).
3. Change the simulation date to **December 21** (Winter Solstice) to test the worst-case scenario for natural light.
4. Convert the building into a greenhouse by setting the Window-to-Wall Ratio (WWR) of all the building's walls to **90%**.
5. Run a **Daily Solar Radiation** analysis (which projects a color-coded radiation heat map onto the 3D scene).
6. Take a screenshot of the 3D viewport showing the applied solar radiation heat map and save it to `~/Documents/Energy3D/winter_radiation_map.png`.
7. Save the modified building design as `~/Documents/Energy3D/greenhouse_design.ng3`.
